competencies.competency_text: Describes relationships (including between humans and animals/nature) and traditions (art forms, celebrations, festivals) in the family and community
competencies.justification: Rishi describes and compares four distinct spring festival traditions: Kashmir Tulip Festival, Jammu's spring ritual (mustard and buransh flowers at doorsteps, elders' blessings), Kerala's Vishu (Vishu Kani with amaltas flowers), and Jharkhand's Sarhul (procession to sakhua tree, offerings to Earth and Sun). Students describe their own festival traditions in Write tasks.

competencies.competency_text: Asks questions and makes predictions about simple patterns (season change, food chain, phases of the moon, movement of stars and planets, shapes of trees, plants, leaves, and flowers, rituals, celebrations) observed in the immediate environment
competencies.justification: Season change is the organising pattern of the chapter. Mami explicitly names the spring-after-winter pattern: flowers blooming, grass growing, new leaves appearing. Activity 2 table maps all four seasons to local festivals and foods — requiring students to recognise and document seasonal patterns. Find out task asks students to inquire about seasonal festivals from elders.

competencies.competency_text: Describes the relationship between the natural environment and cultural practices in their immediate environment (nature of work, food, festivals, traditions)
competencies.justification: The entire chapter is architecturally built on this relationship: spring blooming (tulips, buransh, amaltas, sakhua) → spring festivals across four regions. Mami names it: 'Spring is a celebration of nature.' Activity 2 table explicitly encodes nature (seasons) ↔ culture (festivals, foods). Sarhul description in 'Do You Know' box directly links sakhua flowering to ritual offerings to Earth and Sun.

competencies.competency_text: Observes and describes diversity among plants, and birds and animals in their immediate environment (shape, sounds, food habits, growth, habitat)
competencies.justification: Rishi observes and names diverse flowers in the Himalayan village garden: rose, marigold, hibiscus (familiar), tulip (new — Kashmir), buransh/Rhododendron (Jammu), amaltas (Kerala), sakhua (Jharkhand). The chapter's narrative structure requires students to observe and distinguish plant diversity across regional environments.

competencies.competency_text: Observes and describes cultural diversity in their immediate environment (food, clothing, games, different seasons, festivals related to harvest and sowing)
competencies.justification: Cultural diversity spans food (Activity 3 matches 7 festival foods — thekua, pitha, plum cake, gujiya, sadya, holige, seviyan — to 7 festivals across India), clothing (Write task: festival dress), and festivals from four distinct regions. The chapter explicitly teaches that different communities celebrate the same season differently.

competencies.competency_text: Performs simple inquiry related to specific questions independently or in groups
competencies.justification: Write task (Activity 2) requires students to ask elders about seasonal festivals and foods in their locality — a structured intergenerational inquiry. Two Find out tasks extend this: inquiring whether all communities celebrate with food/clothes; finding out about nearby mountains/rivers and relatives in far places.
